_doc: LOCAL-ONLY backlog for the in-place Ralph loop (gitignored; the shell owns task status — the agent must NOT edit this file). Schema: docs/HARNESS.md §8. All tasks are verifiable with NO live paid-API calls.

tasks.title: Green CI baseline on the public checkout
tasks.do: Make the test suite + typecheck pass on a CLEAN PUBLIC checkout (only public files present — most jobs are still gitignored at this point, so the CI runner sees only the framework + demo job). registry.test.ts currently asserts hardcoded counts (9 jobs / 4 services / 2 pipelines) that only hold when the private jobs are present locally — make it robust to whatever public fileset exists (assert the demo job loads + the registry validates without throwing, not exact private counts). Fix any module that does real work / needs data/ at import time so importing it in CI doesn't fail.
tasks.doneWhen: On a checkout containing only tracked files, `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test` both pass; GitHub Actions 'CI' is green on the pushed commit.

tasks.title: Self-contain the perfume profile template
tasks.do: The repo must be self-contained — it must NOT reference other folders on the machine. perfumes/config.ts hardcodes templatePath='/Users/ryankrol/Development/perfume-markdown/research/_TEMPLATE.md' (leaks the machine's topography). Copy that template's CONTENT into a tracked in-project file src/jobs/perfumes/profile.template.md, change config.ts to resolve it RELATIVE to the perfumes job dir (no absolute path, no external repo), with an optional PERFUMES_TEMPLATE_PATH env override defaulting to the in-project file. Keep build.ts's FALLBACK_TEMPLATE. Document PERFUMES_TEMPLATE_PATH in .env.example. NOTE: the perfumes folder is still gitignored at this point — make the edits in place; the actual publish happens in T004.
tasks.doneWhen: No absolute/external-repo path remains in any job code (`grep -rn '/Users/' src/jobs` is clean except inside data/); perfumes build still loads a template; typecheck + tests pass.

tasks.title: Publish the places job code
tasks.do: Make the places job CODE public. Remove ONLY the 'src/jobs/places/' line from .gitignore (keep 'src/jobs/**/data/' and all secret rules). `git add` the places .ts files EXPLICITLY by path (NEVER `git add -A`); confirm via `git status` that nothing under src/jobs/places/data/ is staged. Verify a clean checkout still builds: the newly-public code must typecheck and the suite must stay green with no data/ present (use fixtures, no live API). Update README's note that 'jobs stay private' to reflect that places is now public.
tasks.doneWhen: src/jobs/places/*.ts is tracked; src/jobs/places/data/** remains ignored (verify with `git check-ignore src/jobs/places/data/out`); typecheck + tests green; the pre-push guard would pass (no sensitive path staged).

tasks.title: Publish the perfumes job code
tasks.do: Make the perfumes job CODE public (depends on T002 having removed the external template path). Remove ONLY the 'src/jobs/perfumes/' line from .gitignore (keep data/ + secret rules). `git add` the perfumes .ts files + profile.template.md EXPLICITLY; confirm nothing under src/jobs/perfumes/data/ (especially chrome-profile/) is staged. Note in docs/LIMITATIONS.md that publishing exposes Fragrantica-scraping technique (accepted).
tasks.doneWhen: src/jobs/perfumes/*.ts + profile.template.md tracked; src/jobs/perfumes/data/** (incl. chrome-profile) still ignored; typecheck + tests green; no sensitive path staged.

tasks.title: Unit tests for the framework core
tasks.do: Build out unit-test coverage for the framework core (currently only dag.ts + store helpers are tested). Cover: the executor (timeout-kill, retries, event parsing), the pipeline-executor (skip/aggregation/repeat-until-stable paths), the scheduler (croner triggers respect `enabled`), the service limiter callService (quota + throttle + min-interval spacing), and the notifier's header sanitiser. Use fake timers / injected clocks and stub child processes / network — NO live external calls. Keep tests against the scratch DB only.
tasks.doneWhen: New *.test.ts files exercise the above paths; `npm test` is green; the new suites meaningfully assert behaviour (not just smoke imports).

tasks.title: Validation gates between pipeline stages
tasks.do: Add typed-artifact validation gates between dependent pipeline stages: let a job declare what it `produces`/`consumes`, and have the DAG/pipeline executor validate the contract at each boundary so an external-format drift (Takeout CSV layout, Fragrantica page structure) fails LOUD at the exact gate instead of passing bad data downstream. Make a failed gate a first-class, surfaced failure (run status + notification). Unit-test both a passing contract and a drifted/failing one.
tasks.doneWhen: Contracts are declarable + enforced between stages; a violation produces a clear surfaced failure; tests cover pass + fail; typecheck + tests green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Docs audit — README + CLAUDE.md vs current repo
tasks.do: Audit README.md + CLAUDE.md against the current repo state and fix drift: the pipelines + services model, the ntfy notify topic/config, the work-ledger / usage-meter / stuck-items / notifier features, the dashboard pages list, the file map, and the now-PUBLIC places/perfumes jobs (the old 'all real jobs are gitignored' framing is no longer fully true — correct it honestly). Keep claims matched to code.
tasks.doneWhen: Both docs accurately describe the current architecture, jobs, features, and public/private split; no stale claims remain.

tasks.title: Dashboard quick fixes
tasks.do: Four small, self-contained dashboard fixes: (1) the pipeline detail graph (/pipelines/[name]) should show STRUCTURE only — omit the node status label when no `statusByJob` is provided (Dag.tsx); (2) when viewing a stage's logs from a pipeline run (/runs/[id] with a pipeline_run_id), the top-left back-link should return to /pipeline-runs/[pipeline_run_id], not the standalone job; (3) make the top-left 'Local Jobs' wordmark a link to the overview; (4) the overview 'Recent runs' list should show recent PIPELINE runs (link to /pipeline-runs/[id]).
tasks.doneWhen: All four behaviours implemented; `npm --prefix dashboard run build` passes; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Perfumes: populate accord percentages
tasks.do: The parse stage emits accords: [{name, pct}] but pct is always null. Pull each accord's strength (the coloured bar's width %) off the cached Fragrantica page so accords carry weight. Parse against the already-fetched HTML under src/jobs/perfumes/data — NO live scrape.
tasks.doneWhen: Accords carry a numeric pct parsed from the bar width on a cached fixture; pct is null only when genuinely absent on the page; a unit test asserts the parsed percentages against a committed/synthetic HTML fixture.

tasks.title: Perfumes: handle empty Fragrantica notes sections in markdown
tasks.do: Some Fragrantica notes-pyramid sections come back empty. Decide and implement how that is reflected in the built markdown — handle honestly: don't fabricate notes, don't silently drop the section. Verify against cached/synthetic fixtures, no live scrape.
tasks.doneWhen: An empty notes-pyramid section produces a clear, honest markdown result (section omitted or explicitly marked empty — never fabricated); a unit test covers an empty-section fixture and a populated one.

tasks.title: Perfumes: weight Fragrantica vs LLM research by confidence
tasks.do: Niche houses have low vote counts = low-confidence community data. In the build step, down-weight low-sample Fragrantica signal against the LLM's own web research using a CONTINUOUS confidence weight (e.g. votes/(votes+k)). ALSO compute the ecosystem-wide vote/weight distribution across the scraped Fragrantica corpus to calibrate what counts as high vs low weight, and feed that distribution in as an input. Make the weighting explicit in the output. Verify against cached fixtures, no live calls.
tasks.doneWhen: Build blends Fragrantica vs LLM via a continuous sample-size confidence weight calibrated against the corpus-wide distribution; the chosen weighting is explicit in the built profile; unit tests cover a low-sample and a high-sample fixture and assert the relative weighting.

tasks.title: Places: daily schedule + daily spend cap
tasks.do: The places pipeline is schedule:null (manual-only). Give it a real DAILY cron schedule ('0 3 * * *') so no pipeline is manual-only, and ensure a daily spend cap derived from the monthly free Places allowance (~ monthly/30) is enforced so a daily run can't blow the month. Env-overridable per the caps convention. No live API calls in verification.
tasks.doneWhen: Places pipeline schedule is a daily cron (not null); a daily cap (≈ monthly free allowance / 30) is configured and enforced in the enrich loops; a unit test asserts the cap arithmetic / that the loop stops at the daily cap; config is env-overridable.

tasks.title: Finish service migration: drop per-job caps + backfill service_usage
tasks.do: Remove the redundant per-job capStatus/recordUsage from places-enrich (google-places) and enrich-with-llm (gemini) so the service-level quota is the SINGLE source of truth and the QuotaExceededError soft-fail path is genuinely live (currently shadowed by the job cap). THEN write a backfill script that tops up THIS MONTH's service_usage from job_usage (google-places←places-enrich, gemini←enrich-with-llm) WITHOUT double-counting calls already recorded to service_usage (idempotent), run it in-place against the live data/jobs.db, and self-verify. NO live paid API calls — this only reads/writes the local meter tables.
tasks.doneWhen: Per-job capStatus/recordUsage removed from both jobs; service quota is the sole governor and the QuotaExceededError path is exercised by a unit test; the backfill script is committed, was run in-place, and before/after service_usage vs job_usage counts (reconciled, no double-count) are logged to .harness/worklog/T013.md.

tasks.title: Framework: manual prune of orphaned ledger rows
tasks.do: Build a framework feature to prune work_items whose item_key is no longer in a job's current input (the case that orphaned the 3 old-id perfume rows after their ids were corrected). A job exposes its current input key-set; a MANUAL-triggered prune (API/CLI, never automatic) removes work_items not in that set and surfaces what it removed. The user clears the 3 orphaned perfume rows via this feature.
tasks.doneWhen: A manual prune removes work_items whose item_key is absent from the job's current input set and keeps current ones; it is never triggered automatically; the removal is surfaced (API/log); unit tests cover keeps-current + removes-orphaned.

tasks.title: Framework: shared headless-browser helper (src/core/browser.ts)
tasks.do: Promote the Chrome-profile learnings from the perfumes fetch (persistent profile keeps Cloudflare clearance, real-Chrome channel, jittered pacing, rate=reputation-not-detection) into a shared src/core/browser.ts helper, and refactor the perfumes fetch to use it. Behaviour-preserving extraction. NO live fetch in verification.
tasks.doneWhen: A reusable src/core/browser.ts encapsulates persistent-profile + real-Chrome-channel + jittered-pacing launch; perfumes fetch is refactored onto it with no behaviour change; typecheck + tests green without any live browser/network call.

tasks.title: Framework: first-class pipeline progress percentages
tasks.do: Make progress percentage a first-class citizen of the pipeline (today the perfumes pipeline just shows 0%). Give progress sensible denominators and a real-time roll-up from member jobs, so a pipeline run reports meaningful overall progress instead of a flat 0%.
tasks.doneWhen: Pipeline progress is computed from member-job progress with sensible denominators and rolls up in real time; a unit test asserts the roll-up across member stages; the value is persisted/surfaced for the dashboard.

tasks.title: Manually dismiss stuck items
tasks.do: Some items will never process (genuinely bad data). Add a way to MANUALLY mark a stuck item as permanently dismissed so it drops off the dashboard front page. MUST be manual-only, never automatic, and distinct from 'unstick' (which retries). Likely a new dismissed status on work_items plus an API + dashboard control.
tasks.doneWhen: A stuck item can be manually dismissed and then no longer appears in the stuck list/front page; dismissal is never automatic and is distinct from unstick/retry; unit tests cover dismiss + that a dismissed item stays off the stuck list.

tasks.title: Dashboard: edit service rate limits
tasks.do: The Services page is read-only. Add the ability to adjust a service's rate_per_minute / daily_cap / monthly_cap from the UI, persisted to the services table (overriding the code default). Reconcile code-sync vs user-edits the same way the job enabled-toggle does (user override wins / is preserved across code sync).
tasks.doneWhen: Service rate_per_minute / daily_cap / monthly_cap are editable from the Services page and persisted; a code-sync does not clobber a user override (reconciled like the enabled toggle); unit tests cover persistence + the reconcile rule.

tasks.title: Dashboard: read-only DB browser
tasks.do: Add a read-only SQLite table viewer page to the dashboard so the local DB can be browsed without building a feature per query. List tables and page through rows via a strictly read-only API path (no arbitrary mutation, no write SQL). Respect the existing read-only-client model.
tasks.doneWhen: A dashboard page lists the DB tables and shows paged rows via a read-only API; there is no write/mutation path exposed; the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Dashboard: remove the Jobs tab
tasks.do: Pipelines are the unit of work; jobs should only be viewed via their pipeline. Drop the /jobs tab + flat top-level job list, but keep per-job run/log drill-down reachable from the pipeline DAG nodes (standalone jobs as a section there).
tasks.doneWhen: The /jobs flat tab + list is removed; per-job run/log drill-down is still reachable from pipeline DAG nodes; the dashboard build passes with no dangling links to the removed tab.

tasks.title: Dashboard: clickable overview stat tiles that filter
tasks.do: The four overview tiles (Running / Succeeded / Failed runs / Stuck items) should be clickable and filter the lists below to just that category.
tasks.doneWhen: Each of the four tiles is clickable and filters the lists below to its category (with a way to clear the filter); the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Dashboard: collapse the pipeline-run member-runs list
tasks.do: For a repeat-until-stable pipeline (perfumes), each cycle re-runs all stages, so one pipeline run accumulates ~one row per stage per cycle (can be ~100). It is accurate but overwhelming — group/collapse the member-runs list (e.g. latest per stage, or grouped by cycle/stage) rather than a flat 100-row list.
tasks.doneWhen: The pipeline-run member-runs list is grouped/collapsed (e.g. latest per stage per cycle) instead of a flat long list, while still letting you reach each member run; the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Security hardening of dashboard + API
tasks.do: Harden the dashboard + API so remote actors can't abuse the run/toggle endpoints. Tighten CORS (currently '*') to an allowlist/same-origin, confirm the API binds to 127.0.0.1, and guard the mutating run/toggle endpoints (loopback/token check). Keep the read-only dashboard client working. Pairs with the Tailscale remote-access task.
tasks.doneWhen: CORS is no longer '*' (allowlist/same-origin), the API bind address is confirmed loopback (configurable), and the run/toggle endpoints reject unauthorised callers; unit tests cover a disallowed origin and an unauthorised mutation being rejected.

tasks.title: Remote-accessible dashboard via Tailscale
tasks.do: Make the dashboard reachable over a Tailscale tailnet (not the open internet) via `tailscale serve`. Keep the dashboard bound to localhost and keep the API on loopback (127.0.0.1) — NEVER expose the API. Add API-proxying so the dashboard serves its data through its OWN origin (e.g. Next.js rewrites / route handlers): a remote browser talks only to the dashboard, and the dashboard server (on the Mini) talks to 127.0.0.1:4789. Document the one-time `tailscale serve` setup (and that Funnel must stay OFF) in the README. The machine-side tailscale up / tailscale serve is operational; this task delivers the code + docs.
tasks.doneWhen: Dashboard data loads end-to-end when the browser can reach ONLY the dashboard origin (API proxied through the dashboard; API still bound to 127.0.0.1 and never exposed); README documents the `tailscale serve` setup with Funnel off and the T023 guards in front; dashboard build + typecheck pass. (Live tailnet reachability is verified operationally, not in CI.)

tasks.title: Set package versions to 1.0.0
tasks.do: Both packages are pinned at 0.1. Bump the root and dashboard package.json version fields to 1.0.0.
tasks.doneWhen: Both package.json version fields read 1.0.0; typecheck and the dashboard build pass.

tasks.title: Docs audit — end-state sweep of README + CLAUDE.md
tasks.do: Final end-state documentation sweep after all feature work has landed. Re-audit README.md + CLAUDE.md against the SHIPPED repo and fix any drift the per-task lockstep updates missed: the daily places schedule, the consolidated service-level caps (per-job caps gone), the manual ledger-prune + dismiss-stuck-items features, first-class pipeline progress, the shared headless-browser helper, the dashboard changes (no Jobs tab, filtering tiles, collapsed member-runs, read-only DB browser, editable service limits), the Tailscale remote-access + security-hardening model, and the 1.0.0 version. Keep every claim matched to code.
tasks.doneWhen: README.md + CLAUDE.md accurately describe the final shipped architecture, jobs, schedules, caps, dashboard pages, remote-access/security posture, and version; no stale claim from any earlier task remains; typecheck clean.

tasks.title: Validation gates: declare contracts on real jobs so gates fire
tasks.do: T006 built the gate MECHANISM but no real job declares contracts, so zero gates fire. Declare produces/consumes ArtifactContracts on the perfumes + places pipeline stages. Each contract is SHAPE + NON-EMPTY: assert the produced artifact exists, is non-empty, and has its expected fields/columns (e.g. the Takeout CSV has its expected headers; a parsed perfume has name + notes; a resolved place has a place_id) — enough to catch real format drift without brittle full-schema checks. Verify against cached data/ fixtures — NO live calls.
tasks.doneWhen: Perfumes + places stages declare produces/consumes shape+non-empty contracts; gates derive and fire between stages (pipeline start-log gate count > 0); a unit test proves a satisfied contract passes and a drifted/empty one fails the consumer; no live API/scrape in verification.

tasks.title: Validation gates: surface on the DAG graph + failure logs
tasks.do: Represent validation gates on the pipeline DAG graph: render a gate marker on the producer->consumer edge in a pipeline RUN, make a FAILED gate visually distinct (e.g. red), and make the gate-failure run/logs reachable by clicking it. Expose gate state via the API so the dashboard can render it. Gates show only on a pipeline RUN, never on the structure-only /pipelines/[name] view.
tasks.doneWhen: Gates appear on the pipeline-run DAG between producer->consumer; a failed gate is visually distinct and links to its failure logs; gates do NOT show on the structure-only /pipelines/[name] graph; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Per-item progress: emit ctx.progress(i/N) + per-item logs across all jobs
tasks.do: Jobs that process N items don't report progress as they go — e.g. perfumes-find-url works 41 items but never logs `1/41 …` and the run % doesn't advance. Make every item-processing job emit `ctx.progress(i/N*100)` and a per-item log line as it works (sensible denominators), not just a final summary. Apply across ALL such jobs, not just find-url.
tasks.doneWhen: Item-processing jobs emit per-item progress + a per-item log line; the run % advances during the run (verified against cached fixtures / scratch DB, no live calls); covers every item-loop job.

tasks.title: Move the Chrome profile to a shared framework location
tasks.do: The persistent Chrome profile lives inside the perfumes job (src/jobs/perfumes/data/chrome-profile). Move it to a framework-level location — a top-level data/chrome-profile/ resolved in src/core/browser.ts and env-overridable (e.g. LOCALJOBS_CHROME_PROFILE) — so it isn't perfumes-specific and other scrapers (e.g. the places resolver) can reuse the warmed, trusted profile. Keep the new location gitignored; perfumes fetch keeps working against it.
tasks.doneWhen: The Chrome profile path is framework-level (top-level data/chrome-profile, env-overridable) not under src/jobs/perfumes/data; the new path is gitignored; perfumes fetch still uses it; typecheck + tests green.

tasks.title: Trim the README — it's a wall of text
tasks.do: The README is a wall of text. Trim it: REMOVE the ## Layout file-tree entirely, and present the worked-example pipelines compactly/scannably (a short line each, not paragraphs). Keep the essential what-it-is / how-to-run / ports / config content and the dashboard pages list. Aim for a noticeably shorter, scannable doc.
tasks.doneWhen: The Layout file-tree is gone; example pipelines are compact; the core what-it-is / how-to-run / install content remains accurate; the README is materially shorter.

tasks.title: Don't count cancelled runs as failed
tasks.do: Cancelled runs are currently lumped into the overview Failed tile. A cancelled run is a deliberate stop, not a failure. Stop counting cancelled as failed, and give it its OWN clickable Cancelled overview stat tile (filtering like the other tiles), consistent with how status is aggregated elsewhere.
tasks.doneWhen: Cancelled runs no longer count as Failed; a dedicated clickable Cancelled tile shows + filters them; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Dismissed vs ignored: park ignored items, show only on overview
tasks.do: Manually-ignored items (the Ignore action) currently reappear as stuck and inflate stuck counts on the pipelines tab + pipeline/job detail. There is ONE ignore concept (not two): stuck = automatic after repeated failures; ignored = a manual park. Fix the root cause so an ignored item STAYS ignored (ignore persists; the stuck query excludes ignored; a re-run does not resurrect it), is NEVER counted as stuck, and is shown ONLY on the overview via a new fifth Ignored tile (which filters to them).
tasks.doneWhen: Ignored items never count as stuck and don't appear outside the overview; a 5th clickable Ignored overview tile shows + filters them; an ignored item stays ignored across re-runs; unit tests cover persistence + exclusion-from-stuck.

tasks.title: Services: explicit 'remove cap' control
tasks.do: On the Services edit-limits form it's unclear how to clear a cap back to 'no cap' (inputs just show placeholder text). Add an explicit control (e.g. a 'No cap' / clear button per field) that sets the value to no-cap (null) unambiguously, instead of relying on typing/blanking.
tasks.doneWhen: Each limit field has a clear UI way to set it back to no-cap (null); saving persists null; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Services: colour free services green
tasks.do: On the Services page, colour the 'free' service pill green (paid stays as-is) so free vs paid reads at a glance.
tasks.doneWhen: Free-service pills render green; paid unchanged; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Keep cron schedule expressions on one line
tasks.do: Cron schedule expressions (e.g. perfumes `0 2 * * *`) wrap onto two lines even on a wide desktop. Keep the schedule on one line where possible (e.g. white-space:nowrap on the schedule cell/mono span). Keep this in mind for the mobile styling pass too.
tasks.doneWhen: Schedule cron expressions render on a single line on desktop; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Drop standalone jobs — everything is a pipeline
tasks.do: Remove the concept of standalone (non-pipeline) jobs: EVERY job must belong to a pipeline declared in an explicit *.pipeline.ts manifest (a single job = a one-stage pipeline WITH its own manifest — no implicit wrapping). Drop the dashboard Standalone jobs section, the job-level standalone schedule/enable path (pipelines own scheduling), and the registry/scheduler handling for non-member jobs. A job with no manifest is a config error — fail loud at load. Now that demo is gone there are no standalone jobs to migrate.
tasks.doneWhen: No standalone-job code path remains; every job must be declared in a *.pipeline.ts manifest (single-job pipelines included); a job with no manifest fails loudly at load; the dashboard has no Standalone section; scheduler/registry handle only pipeline-driven jobs; typecheck + tests green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Rename 'pipeline' → 'workflow' everywhere
tasks.do: Rename the pipeline concept to workflow everywhere: types (PipelineDefinition->WorkflowDefinition), DB tables (pipelines, pipeline_jobs, pipeline_runs, pipeline_run_logs -> workflow*) with a migration that PRESERVES existing rows, store functions, the discovery convention *.pipeline.ts -> *.workflow.ts (rename the existing local perfumes/places manifest files too, or they won't be discovered), pipeline-executor.ts -> workflow-executor, API routes (/api/pipelines, /pipeline-runs -> /api/workflows, /workflow-runs), dashboard routes/pages + all labels, the pipeline field on jobs, and all docs. Clean break — no old-name redirects needed. Mechanical but wide. Do this AFTER T037 so the model lands clean.
tasks.doneWhen: No 'pipeline' naming remains for the concept (code, DB tables, routes, UI, docs, *.workflow.ts files); a DB migration moves existing pipeline_* data to workflow_* without loss; typecheck + tests + dashboard build green; docs updated.

tasks.title: Programmatic input ingestion into a pipeline's data/raw
tasks.do: Add a way to push fresh input into a pipeline's data/raw without manual file copying. The mechanism (API endpoint vs CLI vs pull-from-source) is deferred to a human decision before building.
tasks.doneWhen: Mechanism decided with the human, then built so fresh input can be pushed into a pipeline's data/raw without manual copying.

tasks.title: Mobile dashboard styling pass
tasks.do: Make the dashboard responsive on mobile. Write an automated check that loads each dashboard page in a headless browser at an iPhone-17-class viewport (~402px wide) against a scratch DB and asserts basic mobile styling: no horizontal overflow, no text crossing element boundaries, no weird truncation/clipping. The in-place agent RUNS this check locally and records the per-page result in its worklog — it does NOT need to be added to CI. Then fix the responsive CSS (tables, stat cards, nav, DAG graph, schedule cells) until it passes.
tasks.doneWhen: An automated iPhone-17-viewport (~402px) check covers every dashboard page and asserts no horizontal overflow / no boundary-crossing / no truncation; the agent ran it locally and recorded the page-by-page outcome in .harness/worklog/T040.md; the dashboard passes it; dashboard build green.

tasks.title: Decide + wire up where pipeline output goes
tasks.do: Decide where pipeline output ultimately lands (Obsidian vault / static site / cloud sync) and build the step that delivers it there, instead of leaving output in data/out. The destination is a human decision first.
tasks.doneWhen: Destination decided with the human; the delivery step is built and writes output there.

tasks.title: More intuitive remote dashboard access (alias)
tasks.do: Make remote dashboard access friendlier than typing the tailnet IP:port on mobile — a Tailscale MagicDNS hostname, a `tailscale serve` nice URL, or a phone home-screen shortcut. Partly operational (Tailscale admin / the machine), not CI-verifiable.
tasks.doneWhen: The dashboard is reachable from the phone via a friendly alias/shortcut (human confirms).

tasks.title: Joint review of the perfumes pipeline
tasks.do: Walk through how the (overnight-built) perfumes pipeline actually works, together — it hasn't been reviewed jointly. A conversation, not a build.
tasks.doneWhen: The perfumes pipeline has been walked through together and any follow-ups captured.

tasks.title: Folder-hierarchy check-in
tasks.do: Review the repo's file/folder organisation together and confirm it's laid out the way the owner wants. Subjective, no pass/fail.
tasks.doneWhen: Repo layout reviewed together and confirmed / adjustments captured.

tasks.title: Dashboard styling — make it more joyful
tasks.do: Check in on the dashboard's overall look & feel and make it more joyful. Subjective design work needing the owner's eye.
tasks.doneWhen: Look & feel reviewed with the owner and agreed changes applied.

tasks.title: Investigate why the Claude CLI service has a per-minute rate limit
tasks.do: Investigate why the claude-cli service is configured with a per-minute rate limit (rate_per_minute). Work out whether that cap is actually necessary/correct — is the Claude CLI genuinely rate-limited per minute, or is the value arbitrary/leftover? Then decide whether to keep, adjust, or remove it.
tasks.doneWhen: The reason for the claude-cli per-minute rate limit is understood and a decision is recorded (keep / adjust / remove).

tasks.title: Backlog view: green done icon + in-progress indicator
tasks.do: On the dashboard Backlog page (/backlog), make the done status badge/icon GREEN (it currently renders neutral), and add a distinct IN-PROGRESS icon. Note: TASKS.json only has pending/done (no in_progress status), so derive in-progress client-side as the single next-eligible task — the first not-done task in array order whose dependsOn are all done and which is not gated (i.e. the one the loop builds next) — and badge it distinctly from plain pending. (If a richer live currently-building signal is wired later, prefer that.)
tasks.doneWhen: Done tasks show a green done badge/icon on /backlog; the next-eligible task shows a distinct in-progress icon; plain pending tasks stay neutral; the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Overview: hide the Stuck-items section when empty
tasks.do: On the overview page, hide the entire "Stuck items" SECTION (its heading + list/empty-state) when there are zero stuck items — render the section only when there is at least one stuck item. Keep the Stuck stat TILE/count at the top regardless. Apply the same empty-section hiding to the Ignored section if it has its own dedicated section.
tasks.doneWhen: When nothing is stuck, the overview shows no "Stuck items" heading or section (just the count tile up top); when something is stuck the section renders as before; the dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Sweep the repo for lingering "pipeline" usage (rename to workflow)
tasks.do: After the pipeline→workflow rename (T038), do a COMPLETE repo scan for any remaining use of the word "pipeline" and convert it to "workflow" across the board — code identifiers, types, API routes, UI labels/headings, comments, and docs (README.md / CLAUDE.md / docs) / schema. EXCLUDE legitimate residual references: the DB migration that renames the old `pipelines`/`pipeline_*` tables MUST still name them to perform the rename — leave those; and node_modules, .git, data/, and historical .harness/worklog/ notes are out of scope.
tasks.doneWhen: A `grep -ri pipeline` over tracked source + docs (excluding the migration’s necessary old-table references, node_modules, data/, and worklog) returns nothing; every stray identifier/label/route now reads "workflow"; typecheck + tests + dashboard build pass.

tasks.title: Workflows page: get the Run button onto one line
tasks.do: On the workflows page the ▶ Run button is chunky — the play icon and the word "Run" wrap onto two lines. Make it render on a SINGLE line (e.g. white-space:nowrap / inline-flex / tighter padding / smaller icon — styling is open, the user is not opinionated). Apply the same fix to any other Run button that wraps (e.g. the workflow cards on the overview).
tasks.doneWhen: The Run button renders on one line (icon + label, no wrap) on the workflows page (and any other card where it wrapped); dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Workflows page: clean up the last-run column (pill + time)
tasks.do: On the workflows page, the last-run column shows a status pill (running/success) plus a relative time (e.g. "8 minutes ago") that currently wraps awkwardly across two lines. Make it clean: EITHER force the pill + time onto one line (white-space:nowrap), OR stack them deliberately (pill on its own line, time on the next). Pick whichever reads best — the user is fine with either as long as it is not an awkward wrap.
tasks.doneWhen: The last-run cell renders cleanly (pill + time on one line, or deliberately stacked) with no awkward mid-value wrap; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Workflows page: centre the Stages + Schedule column values
tasks.do: On the workflows page table, centre-align the VALUES in the "Stages" and "Schedule" columns (they read better centred). Leave the "Last run" and "Next" column values LEFT-aligned as they are. Centre the matching headers too if that reads cleaner.
tasks.doneWhen: Stages + Schedule column values (and ideally their headers) are centre-aligned on the workflows page; Last run + Next stay left-aligned; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Database view: surface useful canned queries (not a SQL editor)
tasks.do: The Database view currently only does full per-table scans, which are hard to parse. Add a set of useful PREDEFINED/canned queries surfaced on that page — NOT a free-form SQL editor — covering the most useful read-only views, e.g.: recent failed runs, stuck + ignored items by job, service usage/spend this month vs caps, workflow-run durations/outcomes, work_items counts by status per job. Implement them as NAMED, server-side, strictly read-only (SELECT-only) queries — the same safety model as the existing table browser, never arbitrary SQL from the client — exposed via the API and rendered on the DB page alongside the table browser.
tasks.doneWhen: The Database view offers a set of named common queries (read-only) with results rendered; no free-form/arbitrary SQL is accepted from the client; the existing table browser still works; a unit test covers each canned query against the scratch DB; typecheck + tests + dashboard build pass.

tasks.title: Database view: click a row to see full row data (truncation fix)
tasks.do: On the Database view table browser, long cell values (especially the message column on e.g. workflow-run logs) are truncated with no way to read the full value. Make each row clickable to open a popover/modal showing the COMPLETE row data — every column, untruncated (a key/value list or pretty-printed JSON). The full row data is already returned by the read-only API; this is purely a display affordance, closeable (Esc / click-away).
tasks.doneWhen: Clicking a row in the DB table browser opens a popover/modal showing the full untruncated value of every column; it is dismissable; the table browser otherwise works as before; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Backlog view: show each task’s escalation path
tasks.do: On the Backlog view, show each task’s ESCALATION PATH (the model/effort ladder the loop climbs on repeated failure), not just the starting model. Render the EFFECTIVE ladder — rung 0 (task model/effort, else defaults) then each escalation rung — resolving per-task `escalation` else `defaults.escalation` (same resolution the loop’s task_ladder does). This needs `/api/backlog` to ALSO return the backlog `defaults` (it currently returns only `tasks`) so inherited model/escalation can be resolved, and the BacklogTask type to include `escalation`. Also: where a task INHERITS the default model/effort, show the ACTUAL resolved value (e.g. "opus/high"), never the literal word "default" — so the view always reflects the real current default and updates automatically if `defaults` changes.
tasks.doneWhen: Each task on /backlog shows its escalation path (e.g. "sonnet/medium → opus/high") resolving per-task vs defaults inheritance; a task that inherits the default shows the REAL resolved model/effort (never the word "default"); a task with no escalation shows just its single rung; the API returns defaults; dashboard build + typecheck pass.

tasks.title: Overview: workflow status pill links to that run
tasks.do: On the overview page, each workflow card shows a status pill (running / success / cancelled / …) for its last/current run, but there is no one-click way to reach that run — you have to click the workflow, find the run, then Details. Make the pill a LINK straight to that specific workflow run (/workflow-runs/{last_run.id}) — i.e. whichever run it references (the currently-running one, else the most recent). The run id is already on the card’s last_run.
tasks.doneWhen: On the overview, clicking a workflow card’s status pill navigates directly to that workflow run’s detail page in one click; a card with no run yet shows a non-link pill (no dead link); dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Validation gates: clickable detail + comprehensive logging
tasks.do: Validation gates now render on a workflow run (T028) but you cannot INSPECT them — no way to see what a gate validated, its logs, or to manually double-check it. Add comprehensive gate logging + clickable gate detail: (1) the workflow executor logs, per gate check, the contract key, producer→consumer, what it ASSERTED (the contract description) and the pass/fail result + any violation detail, into the workflow run’s framework logs; (2) expose per-gate detail via the API (contract key + description, state, and the relevant member-run/log refs); (3) make EVERY gate on the run DAG clickable to a detail view showing what it validates and its outcome — passing and pending gates too, not only failed ones (T028) — so you can confirm a gate is doing what you expect.
tasks.doneWhen: Each validation gate on a workflow run is clickable and opens a detail view showing what it validates (contract key + description, producer→consumer) and its outcome; the executor logs each gate check (assertion + result/violation) to the run’s framework logs; passing/pending gates are inspectable, not just failed ones; a unit test covers the gate logging/detail; typecheck + tests + dashboard build pass.

tasks.title: Back-nav: drop "workflow run" words, show just the run id
tasks.do: When you open a job/run from within a workflow run, the back-nav header reads "← workflow run {id}" (lowercase). Drop the words "workflow run" — show just the run id (a leading "#" is fine, e.g. "← #42"). Update both the backFrom helper’s workflow-run label (ui.tsx) and the runs/[id] fallback that also builds "workflow run {id}".
tasks.doneWhen: The back-link to a workflow run shows just the run id (no "workflow run" words); both the ?from-derived label and the runs/[id] fallback are updated; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Status labels: consistent success/failure wording (parity)
tasks.do: Status terminology lacks parity: the UI shows success as "OK" in places, which does not pair with "failed". Standardize the human-facing success/failure labels across the WHOLE dashboard to a consistent pair — use "Succeeded" / "Failed" (matching the existing overview tile) everywhere a run/gate status is shown (StatusBadge, status pills, gate states, etc.), and keep the other labels in consistent tense (Running / Cancelled / Pending). Best done by centralizing a statusLabel() map in ui.tsx and using it at every render site rather than printing the raw status. DISPLAY ONLY — do NOT change the underlying status VALUES ("success"/"failed"/"cancelled"/… in the types / DB / API), only how they render.
tasks.doneWhen: Success renders consistently as "Succeeded" (no stray "OK") and failure as "Failed" across every dashboard view; other statuses read in consistent tense; underlying status values unchanged; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Backlog view: add a Done section
tasks.do: On the Backlog view (/backlog), add a THIRD section "Done" alongside "Harness-buildable" and "Needs a human", listing all completed (status:done) tasks so the page shows a complete history of work done. Done tasks must appear ONLY under Done — the Harness-buildable and Needs-a-human sections show only NOT-done tasks. Order Done by id; it will be long (~40+), so keep it compact/scannable (a one-line row per task; collapsing it by default is fine).
tasks.doneWhen: /backlog shows three sections — Harness-buildable, Needs a human, Done; done tasks appear only under Done (not under buildable); the other two sections list only pending tasks; the section counts are correct; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Backlog view: a DAG / dependency graph of the tasks
tasks.do: Add a DAG / graph view of the backlog on /backlog that shows the task dependency hierarchy visually. Each node is the task ID; lay nodes out in dependency (topological) order with edges for `dependsOn` — the existing components/Dag.tsx wave-layout can be reused/generalized. Colour nodes by status (done / pending / needs-human). On hover (or click) over a node, show more detail about that task — title, model + escalation, status, and the do/doneWhen summary. Offer it alongside the existing list (a toggle, or a second section). Data is already in /api/backlog (tasks + dependsOn).
tasks.doneWhen: /backlog offers a DAG view: nodes labelled by task id, edges for dependsOn, nodes coloured by status, and per-node detail on hover/click; the existing list view still works; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Daily self-inspection workflow: review run logs → propose/fix framework issues
tasks.do: Add a workflow that runs ONCE PER DAY and inspects the new logs produced by other workflows runs since the last inspection (run_logs / workflow framework logs on the box), evaluates any errors for FRAMEWORK-LEVEL problems (not job-specific bad-data), and — where a fix is safe — proposes and applies it. Envisioned as a one-step workflow whose single job instructs Claude (headless) to read the recent logs and propose fixes. NEEDS-HUMAN: discuss the design first — how Claude runs autonomously and safely APPLIES/commits framework fixes (vs just proposing), how to distinguish framework-level vs job-level errors, how it interacts with the build loop / CI / the always-push rule, guardrails against runaway or unreviewed changes, and how findings are surfaced (dashboard / notification / a drafted task in the backlog).
tasks.doneWhen: Design agreed with the human; then a daily workflow reviews new run logs, identifies framework-level errors, and proposes (and where safe, applies) fixes within the agreed guardrails.

tasks.title: Validation gate: open a dedicated page (not an in-page scroll)
tasks.do: On a workflow run, clicking a validation gate currently just SCROLLS to an in-page "Validation Gates" section. Make it behave like clicking a job node — navigate to its OWN dedicated page (e.g. a /workflow-runs/[id]/gates/[key] route, or /gates/...) that shows that gate in full: what it validates (contract key + description), the two stages it bridges, its state, and its logs. Refactor the in-page section into that dedicated page + route (add a read-only API route for a single gate if needed).
tasks.doneWhen: Clicking a validation gate on a workflow run navigates to a dedicated gate-detail PAGE (not an in-page scroll), consistent with how a job node opens its own page; the page shows the gate contract/description, the stages it bridges, state, and logs; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Validation gates: reframe producer/consumer as input/output
tasks.do: The validation-gate detail/logs label the two sides "producer" and "consumer", which is jargon-y. Reframe as INPUT/OUTPUT: the producer side validates the upstream stage’s OUTPUT; the consumer side validates the downstream stage’s INPUT. Relabel the UI and the gate-check log lines accordingly — e.g. "output (from <stage>)" / "input (to <stage>)" — so it reads intuitively. Display/wording only; keep the underlying produces/consumes contract model + field names unchanged.
tasks.doneWhen: Gate detail + gate-check log lines read in input/output terms instead of producer/consumer; the underlying contract data model is unchanged; dashboard build + any affected tests pass.

tasks.title: Validation gate: visual representation of what is being checked
tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): Design for a BLACK BOX — a user must understand the data from the dashboard alone, with no knowledge of system internals. Show the contract's expected shape AND a small sample/summary of the ACTUAL artifact that flowed, with pass/fail per expectation, worded for a non-expert reader.
tasks.do: On the validation-gate page the producer/consumer log lines do not make it obvious WHAT the gate checks. Add a clear, VISUAL representation of the contract: a left-to-right flow — upstream stage OUTPUT → [the gate: what it validates] → downstream stage INPUT — so you can see at a glance what data the gate expects and whether it is satisfied. Surface the contract’s expected shape (key + description + the fields/columns/non-empty it asserts) and, where feasible, a small sample/summary of the ACTUAL artifact that flowed, with pass/fail per expectation. This likely needs the produces/consumes ArtifactContract to optionally declare a machine-readable expected shape (fields/columns) for display, and the gate to report expected-vs-actual rather than just ok/violations. Builds on T063 (dedicated gate page) + T064 (input/output framing).
tasks.doneWhen: The gate page visually shows, left-to-right, output → gate(what it checks) → input, making it obvious what the gate validates and what actually flowed (expected shape + a sample/summary + pass/fail per expectation); contracts can declare an expected shape for display; typecheck + tests + dashboard build pass.

tasks.title: Workflow-run DAG: fix the graph layout on mobile/narrow widths
tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): Switch to a TOP-DOWN (vertical) DAG layout below the mobile breakpoint — don't just re-centre the left-to-right layout. Validate at ~402px AND desktop via viewport screenshots recorded in the worklog.
tasks.do: On a workflow-run page, the DAG graph (components/Dag.tsx — left-to-right waves) looks misaligned on narrow/mobile widths: the vertical centring of nodes/waves reads as visually off as the width shrinks. Make it responsive — EITHER fix the vertical alignment of the left-to-right layout at narrow widths, OR switch to a TOP-DOWN (vertical) layout below a small-screen breakpoint (lean: top-down likely reads better on a phone since multi-node waves do not fit horizontally). The agent picks whichever looks best. Validate visually: render the page at an iPhone-class viewport (~402px) AND at desktop width and confirm the graph is cleanly aligned + legible at both (reuse the T040 viewport-screenshot approach; screenshot-verifiable, not needs-human).
tasks.doneWhen: The workflow-run DAG renders cleanly aligned + legible at a narrow/mobile viewport (~402px) and at desktop width, with no awkward vertical-centring misalignment; validated via viewport screenshots at both widths recorded in the worklog; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Backlog view: make every section collapsible
tasks.do: On the Backlog view (/backlog), make EACH section collapsible — currently only the Done section can collapse. Apply the same collapse/expand toggle to the Harness-buildable and Needs-a-human sections (and any others), each with its count in the header, so the whole page can be tidied. Match the existing Done-section collapse pattern; each section toggles independently.
tasks.doneWhen: On /backlog, every section (Harness-buildable, Needs a human, Done) has a working collapse/expand toggle with its count; sections toggle independently; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Workflow-run view: show only the latest member run per stage
tasks.do: On a workflow-run page, the Member runs section shows one row per stage PLUS a collapsible (▼ +N) of EARLIER-CYCLE runs — because a repeat-until-stable workflow re-runs every stage each cycle, so one run accumulates multiple runs per stage. Remove those earlier-cycle rows and the show/hide toggle from this view: show exactly ONE row per stage (the latest run for that stage in this workflow run). The earlier-cycle runs stay in the DB and remain reachable via the job/stage own run history, so nothing is lost — they just stop cluttering the per-run view.
tasks.doneWhen: The workflow-run Member runs section shows a single row per stage (the latest run for that stage in this run); the earlier-cycle (▼ +N / ▲ hide) expander is gone; earlier-cycle runs remain in the DB and reachable elsewhere; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Make the DAG prettier — outlined/boxed job nodes
tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): Style = subtle bordered cards: 1px border, rounded corners, padding, and a COLOURED STATUS ACCENT (a left accent bar or dot) — NOT whole-card status tinting. Keep it understated and scannable.
tasks.do: Make the DAG (components/Dag.tsx) prettier and easier to read. Right now nodes are essentially flat text, which is hard to scan when there are many. Give each job node a clear OUTLINE/box (bordered card — padding, rounded corners), make the connecting arrows/edges read as a flow, and keep the status colouring legible. Use your own judgement on the exact styling; the goal is a visually pleasing, scannable graph. Applies wherever the DAG renders (the workflow definition page and the workflow-run page).
tasks.doneWhen: DAG job nodes render as outlined/boxed cards (not flat text) with clear flow edges and legible status colours, noticeably more scannable on both the workflow definition and workflow-run pages; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Strip workflow-level properties from individual jobs
tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): Remove ALL job-level properties — schedule, enabled toggle, next-run, the instructions panel, AND the per-job Run-now button. The job page becomes a read-only member view (status, run history, logs). You run a workflow, never a job.
tasks.do: A job is only ever a member of a workflow now (T037), so workflow-level concerns must NOT live on individual jobs. On the job view (/jobs/[name]) strip: the Schedule, the Enabled toggle, the Next run, the How-to-run-this-job instructions panel, and the Run-now button — you run a WORKFLOW, not a job; a job runs when its prerequisites are met inside its workflow. Remove these properties at the job level wherever they are integrated (the JobDefinition type, jobs-table columns + a migration, the scheduler per-job scheduling path, the API job view, the dashboard job page). Workflows keep schedule/enabled/run/next-run; the job page becomes a read-only member view (status, run history, logs). This may be a comprehensive refactor — that is acceptable.
tasks.doneWhen: Individual jobs no longer carry schedule/enabled/next-run/instructions and have no Run-now button or per-job scheduling; those live only at the workflow level; /jobs/[name] is a read-only member view; a DB migration drops the now-unused job columns without loss; typecheck + tests + dashboard build pass; docs updated.

tasks.title: Show validation gates on the workflow definition graph
tasks.do: On the workflow DEFINITION page (/workflows/[name]) the graph does not show validation gates at all — they currently only appear on a workflow RUN (with per-run state). Represent the gates STRUCTURALLY on the definition graph too: derive the workflow gates from the produces/consumes contracts on its member jobs (the same deriveGates the executor + run view use, already available via gatesForWorkflow) and mark them on the relevant producer-to-consumer edges, WITHOUT run state — just that a validation gate exists there, ideally clickable to its contract detail. Expose gates on the workflow-definition API endpoint if needed.
tasks.doneWhen: The /workflows/[name] definition graph shows a marker on every edge that carries a validation gate (structural, no run state), consistent with how gates render on a run; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Fragrantica accord percentages still null — evaluate + fix end-to-end
tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): ROOT CAUSE — fetch.ts persists only innerText (pages/<id>.txt); the accord bar widths live in raw HTML that is saved ONLY for failed pages (pages-failed/<id>.html). T009's parser reads pages/<id>.html, which never exists on the success path, so pct always falls back to null. FIX: extend fetch.ts to ALSO persist the raw HTML alongside the .txt so widths survive going forward (keep the .txt for the Claude parse step). The committed unit test stays hermetic on a synthetic HTML fixture (do NOT commit real Fragrantica HTML). END-TO-END proof is for the agent's own confidence: ONE local live fetch of a single perfume (paced via the fragrantica service) is allowed to confirm fetch.ts persists HTML and parse fills real pcts — be frugal; the monthly cap is the only hard ceiling. The existing 42 outputs stay null until re-fetched in production.
tasks.do: Despite T009, the perfumes output still has accords with pct=null (example: Amouage Beach Hut Man — every accord pct is null). Think deeply and evaluate END-TO-END whether accurate accord percentages can be extracted: from the Fragrantica page HTML (the coloured accord-bar widths, expressed as a width % in a style attribute) or, failing that, another signal. Work out WHY T009 still yields null (selector drift? the % lives in a style attr that is not being read?). Fix the parse so accords carry accurate pct. BACKTEST: there is no usable HTML on disk (success-path pages are saved as innerText .txt only — see the design note), so first change fetch.ts to persist the raw HTML, then prove the fix end-to-end against real HTML — ONE frugal local live fetch of a single perfume is acceptable (the monthly cap is the only hard ceiling). Confirm pcts are populated in the OUTPUT files and match the bar widths on the page (spot-check a few). Record before/after in the worklog.
tasks.doneWhen: Perfumes accords carry accurate, non-null percentages (matching the page bar widths) wherever the page provides them; fetch.ts is changed to persist raw HTML so the parser can read bar widths going forward; the parse is fixed + unit-tested against a synthetic HTML fixture; the agent confirms end-to-end (a single frugal local live fetch is acceptable) that the built output now has populated pcts, recorded in the worklog; pct is null ONLY when genuinely absent on the page; typecheck + tests pass.

tasks.title: Backlog view: pending shows pending, not in progress
tasks.design: Clarification (interview 2026-06-22): This was implemented in commit 2e65bbb then reverted in e37e40c; re-apply EXACTLY as that commit did and make it stick — the list shows 'pending' (no '▶ in progress'), drop the next-task list highlight/border, and keep the DAG view's accurately-labelled 'next' node highlight as-is.
tasks.do: On the Backlog LIST view, the next-eligible task is labelled with a "▶ in progress" pill (statusPill), which is misleading — nothing is actively building it (TASKS.json has no in_progress status, and the loop may not even be running). A not-done task should show its REAL status (pending). Show "pending" in the list and drop the next-task highlight/border there. Keep the DAG view’s accurately-labelled "next" node highlight as-is.
tasks.doneWhen: On /backlog a pending task shows "pending" (no "in progress" label) and no special list highlight; the DAG view keeps its "next" node; dashboard build passes.

tasks.title: Services edit-limits: consistent placeholder wording across the three inputs
tasks.do: On the Services page (/services) 'Edit limits' form the three input placeholders are inconsistent: the Rate/min input uses placeholder="none" while Rate/day and Rate/month both use placeholder="no cap" (page.tsx lines ~101/105/109). Make the three placeholders use consistent wording so a blank field reads the same way across all three (e.g. all 'no limit'); keep the existing blank-input-means-null behaviour and the per-field ✕ 'set to no limit/no cap' clear buttons working.
tasks.doneWhen: On the Services edit-limits form, the Rate/min, Rate/day and Rate/month inputs show consistent placeholder text for the empty (no-limit) state; blank still maps to null and the ✕ clear buttons still work; dashboard build passes and the mobile-check stays green.

tasks.title: Database page: add padding inside the Common queries / Browse tables button panels
tasks.do: On the Database page (/db) the 'Common queries' and 'Browse tables' panels render their buttons directly inside a `.panel` with only `display:flex; flexWrap; gap:8` and no padding (page.tsx ~lines 132 and 163), so the buttons sit flush against the panel border (the `.panel` class itself has no padding by design — globals.css:46 — because table panels go edge-to-edge). Add a bit of inner padding to just these two button panels so the buttons aren't stuck to the container boundary. Do NOT add padding to `.panel` globally — that would break the table panels that intentionally fill edge-to-edge; scope the padding to these button containers (inline style or a dedicated class).
tasks.doneWhen: On /db the Common queries and Browse tables button panels have inner padding so the buttons no longer touch the panel border; table-filled panels elsewhere remain edge-to-edge (unchanged); dashboard build passes and the mobile-check stays green.

tasks.title: Backlog DAG view: nicer node rendering + drop the 'next' indicator
tasks.do: The Backlog DAG view (BacklogDag in components/Dag.tsx) renders each task as a bare node showing just the id + a status word, with a plain '→' between waves — it reads as a flat list of plain-text items. Two changes: (1) Make the DAG visually nicer — give nodes proper styled cards (e.g. show the task title, not only the id; clearer status colouring; better node/edge/wave styling) while keeping the horizontal scroll, the click-to-select-and-inspect behaviour, and mobile responsiveness. (2) Remove the 'next' concept from the DAG view entirely: drop the '▶ next' node-status label (Dag.tsx ~line 235), the nextId-driven 'running' colour class (Dag.tsx ~line 218), the '· next' legend entry (page.tsx ~line 157), and stop using nextId for any highlight on this view. NOTE: this supersedes T073's decision to keep the DAG 'next' highlight — update the now-stale comment at page.tsx:30 and the DagDetailPanel isNext styling accordingly.
tasks.doneWhen: On /backlog the DAG view renders tasks as styled nodes (title visible, not bare id + arrow plain text); there is NO 'next' label, highlight, or legend entry anywhere on the DAG view; clicking a node still opens its detail panel; dashboard build passes and mobile-check stays green.

tasks.title: Backlog list: make Done items clickable to reveal task detail
tasks.do: On the Backlog LIST view, items under the 'Done' heading (the DoneRow component, page.tsx ~lines 65-73) are a flat, non-interactive list of id + title + '✓ done' pill. Make each done row clickable to reveal more context about the task — its `do`, `doneWhen`, depends-on, and tags (reuse the existing TaskCard / DagDetailPanel content layout) — e.g. expand inline on click or open a detail panel/modal. Keep the collapsed default list compact (the current one-line-per-task summary) so the Done section stays scannable; show the fuller detail only after a click.
tasks.doneWhen: On /backlog clicking a Done item reveals its fuller detail (do, done-when, depends-on, tags); the default collapsed Done list stays a compact one-line-per-task summary; dashboard build passes and mobile-check stays green.

tasks.title: Backlog section headers: add an expand/collapse caret affordance
tasks.do: The collapsible section headers on the Backlog list view (the <details>/<summary className="section-heading-summary"> blocks in backlog/page.tsx — 'Harness-buildable', 'Needs a human', 'Done') give no visual signal that they're clickable/expandable: there's no caret or marker that changes with state. Add a caret/disclosure indicator on each section header that changes orientation with the open/closed state — collapsed vs expanded (e.g. a caret pointing right ▶ when collapsed and rotating to point down ▼ when open; the user described it as 'left and down', so make the orientation clearly distinct between states). Drive it off the native <details open> state (CSS on .section-heading-summary, e.g. a ::before/::after caret rotated via details[open]); keep it accessible and consistent across all three section headers.
tasks.doneWhen: On /backlog each section header shows a caret that points one way when the section is collapsed and rotates to a clearly different orientation when expanded, making the header obviously clickable; behaviour is consistent across all three sections; dashboard build passes and mobile-check stays green.

tasks.title: Remove the stale job-level Chrome profile; keep only the framework-level one
tasks.do: Two chrome-profile folders exist on disk: the framework-level data/chrome-profile (the one ACTUALLY used — config.ts:19 chromeProfileDir -> browser.ts defaultChromeProfileDir -> perfumes/config.ts:15 profileDir -> fetch.ts:107) and an ORPHANED job-level one at src/jobs/perfumes/data/chrome-profile that NO code references. Keep a single framework-level shared profile: (1) delete the stale local folder src/jobs/perfumes/data/chrome-profile (LOCAL-ONLY cleanup — chrome-profile/ and data/ are gitignored and tripwired by the pre-push guard, so this produces NO tracked diff; never `git add` anything under data/ or chrome-profile/). (2) Because the deletion itself isn't CI-verifiable, the committable deliverable is a regression test locking in the invariant: add a unit test (extend perfumes/config.test.ts) asserting perfumesConfig.profileDir === defaultChromeProfileDir (i.e. the framework-level data/chrome-profile, env-overridable via LOCALJOBS_CHROME_PROFILE) so a job-level profile can't silently creep back in. Confirm grep shows no remaining reference to a job-level profile path.
tasks.doneWhen: src/jobs/perfumes/data/chrome-profile no longer exists locally; the framework-level data/chrome-profile remains and is the only profile any code points at; a unit test asserts perfumesConfig.profileDir === defaultChromeProfileDir; `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test` are green. (No tracked diff is expected from the folder deletion itself — only the new test.)

tasks.title: Mobile workflow DAG: centre the down arrow between stacked node boxes
tasks.do: On a phone-width viewport the workflow graph (DAG) stacks vertically and the between-wave connector becomes a '↓' (rendered via .dag-arrow::after in the @media(max-width:640px) block, globals.css ~lines 268-269). The arrow is not horizontally centred under the node boxes: .dag-arrow keeps display:flex with default left justification (line 123) while the stacked boxes use justify-content:center (.dag-wave, line 266), so the ↓ sits to the left instead of centred between the boxes it connects. Fix the mobile .dag-arrow so the ↓ is horizontally centred relative to the centred node boxes (e.g. give .dag-arrow justify-content:center in the mobile block, or switch it to a centred block). tasks.title: Set maxRetries: 3 on all jobs (retry on any non-success)
tasks.do: All 8 example jobs currently set maxRetries: 0. Set maxRetries: 3 on every job def so a job that finishes in any non-success state (failed or timeout) is retried up to 3 times. tasks.title: Backend: cancel a running workflow run (abort + kill children + API)
tasks.do: Add the ability to cancel/stop a RUNNING workflow run. Today nothing tracks in-flight runs and the execution path is not interruptible. Build the mechanism: (1) IN-MEMORY REGISTRY: keep a Map<workflowRunId, {AbortController}> populated whenever runWorkflow() starts (so BOTH the scheduler path and the manual /api/workflows/:name/run path register) and cleaned up when it settles. (2) ABORT THREADING: thread an AbortSignal from runWorkflow() through executeDag() (dag.ts) and the runOne hook into runJobForWorkflow()/runAttempts()/executeAttempt() (executor.ts). The DAG loop must stop launching new stages once the signal is aborted (skip not-yet-started members); in-flight members must be actively killed. CRITICAL: aborting must ACTUALLY kill the in-flight child process — the abort handler has to call child.kill('SIGTERM') then SIGKILL after a grace period (reuse the existing timeout kill path at executor.ts ~line 112), NOT merely resolve the promise early (that would orphan the child). (3) STORE TRANSITIONS: mark the workflow run 'cancelled' (finishWorkflowRun) and transition member runs sensibly — a killed in-flight member ends 'cancelled', a not-yet-started stage does not spawn. (4) API: add POST /api/workflow-runs/:id/cancel (mutating — use the existing loopback/token authoriseMutation guard), validating the run exists and is 'running', looking it up in the registry, calling abort(), and recording the cancelled state; return { ok: true } (and a clear error if the run is already terminal or not found). Add unit tests for the abort propagation / that an aborted run stops launching stages and marks cancelled. Update CLAUDE.md (executor/workflow-executor architecture + the API route surface) and README (note the new capability) in lockstep. NOTE: this is safety-critical executor code — be careful not to leave processes hanging or break the existing timeout/retry/overlap behaviour.
tasks.doneWhen: POST /api/workflow-runs/:id/cancel aborts a running workflow: in-flight child processes are actually killed (no orphans), no further stages launch, and the workflow run + affected member runs are marked 'cancelled' in the DB; a terminal/unknown run id returns a clear error; existing timeout/retry/overlap behaviour is unchanged; new unit tests cover the abort path; CLAUDE.md + README updated; npx tsc --noEmit and npm test green. (Manually verify mid-run that the child PID is gone and the run shows cancelled.)

tasks.title: Design: workflow run-limits via framework-tracked input lineage
tasks.do: Author a design doc at .harness/designs/T093-workflow-run-limits.md for 'run a workflow capped at N originating inputs'. DECISIONS ALREADY MADE (interview 2026-06-22): (a) N counts ORIGINATING inputs — all fan-out work derived from those N roots runs to completion (limit 1 = one root input plus every descendant it produces, e.g. 1 perfume -> 3 similar -> 9 pages -> 27 files all run); (b) the limit is a MANUAL run-time option only (chosen when clicking Run / via the API; scheduled runs stay unlimited); (c) lineage is FRAMEWORK-TRACKED via a root/lineage key on work_items that the framework threads and enforces across stages, each job declaring each produced item's parent. The doc MUST specify: (1) LINEAGE DATA MODEL — new column(s) on work_items (current PK is (job_name,item_key); add e.g. root_key and/or parent_key) tying every item back to its originating input; how the first stage establishes a root and how downstream jobs declare each produced item's parent so root_key propagates through fan-out; the store.ts API additions (markWorkItem carrying lineage; queries to enumerate/count distinct roots and select the first N pending roots). (2) LIMIT SEMANTICS + SELECTION — with limit N, which N roots are chosen (deterministic, e.g. first N pending) and how each stage filters its input to only items whose root is in the selected set. (3) INVOCATION — extend POST /api/workflows/:name/run with an optional { limit }; the workflow_run record carries the limit; thread the limit + selected root-set through the workflow executor down to each member job. (4) JOB CONTRACT — how a job reads the active root-allowlist and declares item lineage (new JobContext helper and/or JobDefinition field in core/types.ts), and how that reaches the child process (runJob.ts gets the job's run via the executor — env/args). (5) BACKWARD COMPAT — the existing 1:1 places + perfumes workflows keep working unchanged when no limit is set; spell out the minimal change to make them declare lineage (each item's root is its own input). (6) UI — the manual Run control gains an optional limit input. (7) MIGRATION — additive work_items column migration in src/db/index.ts. Keep it concrete enough for the build task (T094) to implement; call out risks and the smallest viable first cut.
tasks.doneWhen: The design doc .harness/designs/T093-workflow-run-limits.md exists and concretely specifies: the lineage data model + work_items schema/column change; the N-originating-inputs limit semantics and root-selection rule; the manual run-time invocation (API + executor + how the root-set/limit reach each job); the JobDefinition/JobContext lineage contract; backward-compat for the existing places/perfumes workflows; and the DB migration. It is detailed enough to implement directly. (Doc-only change; tsc/test trivially green.)

tasks.title: Build: workflow run-limits (input lineage + manual N-input cap)
tasks.do: Implement workflow run-limits per the design doc .harness/designs/T093-workflow-run-limits.md. Agreed shape: framework-tracked input lineage + a MANUAL limit of N originating inputs (all fan-out from each selected root runs to completion; scheduled runs stay unlimited). Concretely: add the lineage key column(s) to work_items (schema.sql) with an additive migration (src/db/index.ts); extend store.ts to record lineage on markWorkItem and to enumerate/select the first N pending roots and filter by root-set; thread an optional { limit } from POST /api/workflows/:name/run through the workflow executor and into each member job (so each stage only processes items whose originating root is in the selected N), reaching the child via runJob.ts; add the JobContext/JobDefinition mechanism (core/types.ts) for a job to read the active root-allowlist and declare each produced item's parent so root_key propagates through fan-out; migrate the existing places + perfumes jobs to declare lineage (1:1, so each item's root is its own input) and confirm they run unchanged with no limit; add an optional limit input to the dashboard Run control. Add unit tests covering lineage propagation, root selection, and limit enforcement INCLUDING a fan-out fixture (1 root -> many descendants) to prove all descendants of the selected roots run. Update CLAUDE.md + README. Follow the design doc; if a decision there proves wrong during implementation, update the doc in the same change. NOTE: this is a large change — if it genuinely cannot fit one context window, record a worklog note proposing a split rather than half-implementing.

tasks.title: Workflow run: show input → output mapping (first stab)
tasks.do: Take a FIRST STAB at surfacing, for a given workflow run, the workflow's input (at the first stage) and output (at the final stage) — ideally mapped to each other, even if it's just a flat list. Approach for this first cut: identify the first-stage and last-stage jobs from the workflow DAG (first wave / last wave via dag.ts), read each job's work_items (item_key + status + detail JSON, which may carry an output path/summary), and join first-stage items to last-stage items by shared item_key to produce a flat list of input-item -> final-output rows. Add a store query + a read-only API (extend GET /api/workflow-runs/:id or add GET /api/workflow-runs/:id/io — must read DB/files only, never a paid/remote call, so it's safe to poll) and render a panel on the workflow-run page showing the input -> output list, linking outputs to something useful (the producing run, or the output file path) where possible. This works for the current 1:1 example workflows (perfumes/places share the item key across all stages). KNOWN LIMITATIONS to call out in the UI/copy and code comments: work_items is global (not scoped per run), so this reflects the workflow's current first/last-stage ledger rather than strictly this run's items, and it does not handle fan-out — precise per-run, lineage-aware mapping arrives with the lineage work (T093/T094); cross-reference those but do NOT hard-depend on them. Keep it a clearly-labelled first cut.
tasks.doneWhen: The workflow-run page shows a panel listing the workflow's input items mapped to their final outputs (a flat input -> output list) derived from the first- and last-stage work_items joined by key, with outputs linking somewhere useful where available; it works for the perfumes and places example workflows; the API path is read-only (DB/files only, safe to poll); the first-cut limitations (not per-run-scoped, no fan-out yet) are noted in the UI and comments; npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, the dashboard build, and mobile-check are all green.

tasks.title: Regression guard: migrations must run against an existing (non-fresh) DB
tasks.do: Prevent the class of bug that just crash-looped the daemon (the T094 regression, fixed in commit 2748c58): a schema-bootstrap statement in schema.sql referencing a column that only a LATER additive migration adds. It was invisible to CI because the unit suite always starts from a FRESH/empty scratch DB — so schema.sql's CREATE TABLE already contained the new column — while the live/existing DB crashed in openDb(). Add a regression test (src/db/migrate-existing-db.test.ts) that exercises the REAL openDb() bootstrap+migration path against a PRE-POPULATED database seeded to an OLDER schema shape: stand up the core tables (work_items, workflow_runs, runs, jobs, services, etc.) in a pre-migration form WITHOUT the newer migration-added columns/indexes, insert a few representative rows, then run the full openDb() path against THAT file and assert it does not throw and ends correctly migrated (expected columns + indexes present, legacy rows backfilled e.g. root_key = item_key). openDb() currently uses config.dbPath and auto-opens at import — do the smallest enabling refactor to make it testable against a given path (e.g. let openDb(path?) accept an optional path) rather than fragile env juggling. Make the test strong enough that it would FAIL against the OLD buggy schema.sql (i.e. it genuinely catches the ordering bug), ideally by seeding from a realistic 'legacy' snapshot so ANY future ordering violation trips it, not just this one. Finally, codify the invariant: add a Conventions note in CLAUDE.md that schema.sql's bootstrap must NOT create an index/constraint on a column added by a later additive migration — such indexes belong IN the migration, after the ALTER (cross-reference the migrateRunLimitLineage pattern in index.ts).
tasks.doneWhen: A regression test runs openDb()'s full bootstrap+migration against a pre-seeded OLD-shape database (tables + rows lacking the newer columns) and asserts openDb does not throw and the DB ends migrated (columns + indexes present, legacy rows backfilled); the test would FAIL if pointed at the pre-fix buggy schema.sql (proven to catch the ordering bug); openDb is invokable against an explicit path via the minimal refactor; CLAUDE.md documents the 'no schema-bootstrap index/constraint on a migration-added column' convention; npx tsc --noEmit and npm test are green.

tasks.title: Enforce one active run per workflow (UI + API + executor)
tasks.do: A workflow must only ever have ONE active run at a time (different workflows MAY still run concurrently — this is per-workflow, not a global lock). Today the Run button stays active on the Workflows list even while that workflow is running. Enforce the constraint at all three layers: (1) UI — disable/relabel the Run button (the per-row button on the Workflows LIST page workflows/page.tsx AND the 'Run now' button on the workflow DETAIL page workflows/[name]/page.tsx) whenever that workflow has an active run (the workflows API already exposes last_run; treat last_run.status === 'running' as active), e.g. show 'Running…' disabled. (2) API — POST /api/workflows/:name/run should reject a duplicate start with a clear response (e.g. 409 Conflict + message) instead of returning ok/202 and silently skipping, so callers know it didn't start. (3) EXECUTOR — the workflow executor is the AUTHORITATIVE guard: it must atomically refuse to start a second concurrent run of the same workflow (there is already a hasActiveWorkflowRun check / overlap-prevention — make it the single source of truth and race-safe, so two near-simultaneous POSTs can't both start). Add unit tests for the API reject and the executor guard (and that a DIFFERENT workflow can still start while one is running). Document the constraint in CLAUDE.md (executor/overlap section) + README.
tasks.doneWhen: Starting a workflow that already has an active run is prevented at every layer: the Run button (list + detail) is disabled/shows 'running' while a run is active; POST /api/workflows/:name/run returns a clear conflict (e.g. 409) rather than appearing to start a second run; the executor atomically refuses a concurrent run of the SAME workflow so a race can't start two, while DIFFERENT workflows can still run concurrently; unit tests cover the API reject + executor guard + the different-workflow case; CLAUDE.md + README document the constraint; npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, the dashboard build, and mobile-check are all green.

tasks.do: Build a NEW workflow that checks my Plex library for TV shows and notifies me when a NEW SEASON has become available (i.e. exists/aired upstream but isn't in my library yet) so I know to download it. NEEDS-HUMAN because it requires my private credentials + infrastructure: a Plex server URL + auth token (PLEX_URL / PLEX_TOKEN), network access to my Plex server, and most likely a TV-metadata source to know which seasons exist (e.g. TVDB/TMDB API key, or a Sonarr instance) — the loop cannot provision these, so the agent should scaffold the workflow around the gap and record failed:blocked until I supply them. Design (follow repo conventions): a multi-stage workflow under a PRIVATE gitignored subfolder src/jobs/plex/ (add 'src/jobs/plex/' to .gitignore; do NOT publish this job's code or document it in the public README) — e.g. stage 1 query the Plex library for each show + its latest owned season; stage 2 look up upstream season availability via the metadata service; stage 3 push a notification (reuse src/core/notifier.ts push() — ntfy + macOS) listing shows with a new season available. Key it idempotently with the work_items ledger per (show, season) so I'm only notified once per new season; route the external metadata calls through a *.service.ts ServiceDefinition for rate-limiting; give the *.workflow.ts a sensible schedule (e.g. daily or weekly). Read all secrets from .env via process.env; add the env var NAMES with placeholders to .env.example (names only, never values). Tell me exactly what to provision (the human step).
