tasks.title: Fix workflow-run stage status flicker + investigate perfumes self-looping
tasks.do: ROOT-CAUSED from the live logs on 2026-06-23 (perfumes run 64cbdf8c). Two related symptoms. (1) SELF-LOOPING / never reaches 'stable': perfumes is repeatUntilStable (maxCycles=40, cycleSleepMs=5000, minAttempts=maxAttempts=4). The cycle stop-condition is workflowRetryableCount(memberNames, minAttempts)==0 (store.ts: COUNT work_items WHERE status='failed' AND attempts < minAttempts). ONE genuinely-unfindable item — perfumes-find-url key 'amouage-jubilation-40', status=failed, attempts=2, detail 'no Fragrantica page found' — is counted as retryable (failed AND attempts<4) EVERY cycle, yet its attempts stay FROZEN at 2 (it is not actually re-attempted/incremented across cycles). So the count is permanently 1, the workflow never settles, and EVERY perfumes run burns all 40 cycles (re-running all 4 stages 40x) for nothing. FIX: make the stop-condition robust — add a NO-FORWARD-PROGRESS early stop (if a whole cycle changes nothing — retryable count didn't drop AND no work_item was updated/attempted this cycle — break instead of spinning to maxCycles), AND/OR align workflowRetryableCount with what will actually be retried (exclude items that won't be re-attempted — e.g. no longer in the job's current input, or effectively unprocessable). Also investigate why perfumes-find-url leaves the item at attempts=2 rather than retrying it up to maxAttempts so it becomes 'stuck' and naturally drops out of the retryable set. (2) STATUS FLICKER: when perfumes runs, each stage first shows 'succeeded', flips to 'running', then back to 'succeeded'. Investigate the per-stage status derivation — statusByJob feeding Dag.tsx + the stage grouping in workflow-runs/[id]/page.tsx may use the LATEST run per job across ALL history (a prior run's success) instead of scoping to THIS run's member runs; also check repeatUntilStable cycle separation and any initial-render/poll race. (3) NOTIFICATION SPAM: the logs show 'notification FAILED (ntfy HTTP 429)' on nearly every stage — per-stage success notifications fire on every cycle (~40x4=160 pushes) and ntfy rate-limits. Suppress/dedupe per-stage notifications during repeatUntilStable cycling (e.g. only notify on the final cycle or on a meaningful state change). Add regression tests for the no-progress stop condition and the status derivation.
tasks.doneWhen: A perfumes run no longer burns all 40 cycles: the repeatUntilStable loop stops once there is no forward progress (a genuinely-unfindable item like amouage-jubilation-40 no longer causes perpetual cycling), proven by a regression test; the per-stage status shows correct states with no initial false 'succeeded' and no succeeded→running→succeeded flicker (scoped to the current run + correct cycle); per-stage notifications no longer spam ntfy during cycling (no 429 storm); regression tests cover the stop-condition + status derivation; npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, and the dashboard build are all green.

tasks.title: ntfy notifications: back off on 429 (exponential, hard-capped) and stop hammering
tasks.do: The ntfy notifications API keeps returning HTTP 429 because we hammered it (e.g. per-stage notifications during repeatUntilStable cycling) and we keep trying anyway. Add backoff to the ntfy sender (sendNtfy in src/core/notifier.ts) so that after a 429 we STOP attempting sends until a cooldown elapses. Use EXPONENTIAL backoff WITH A HARD CAP: on each consecutive 429 increase the cooldown (e.g. start ~30s, double each time, cap at ~10-15 min); HONOR ntfy's Retry-After response header as the cooldown when present (prefer it over the computed value); while within the cooldown window, SKIP the ntfy send entirely (don't even hit the API) and log that it's suppressed; RESET the backoff on the next successful send. Keep the backoff state in-memory in the long-lived daemon process (module-level in notifier.ts — resetting on restart is fine). This must NOT affect the macOS notification path (not rate-limited), and the notifier must still NEVER throw (a broken notifier must not affect job execution). Make the backoff knobs (base, cap) env-overridable via config.ts. Add unit tests: a 429 sets a cooldown, subsequent sends within the window are skipped (no fetch), Retry-After is honored, and a success resets the backoff. NOTE: complements T112 (which reduces per-stage notification volume during cycling) — together they stop the 429 storm.
tasks.doneWhen: After the ntfy API returns 429, further ntfy sends are skipped until a cooldown elapses (exponential backoff with a hard cap, honoring Retry-After when present), and a successful send resets the backoff; the macOS notification path is unaffected and the notifier never throws; backoff base/cap are env-overridable; unit tests cover 429→cooldown→skip→reset (including Retry-After); npx tsc --noEmit and npm test are green.

tasks.title: Reusable stuck-items popover with per-item + bulk unstick/ignore
tasks.do: Clearing stuck items one-by-one is tedious. Build a REUSABLE stuck-items POPOVER/modal that lists stuck items, each with its own ↻ Unstick and ✕ Ignore button, PLUS 'Unstick all' and 'Ignore all' bulk actions (require a CONFIRMATION before a bulk action, especially Ignore-all which permanently parks). Back the bulk actions with store functions in src/db/store.ts (bulk-unstick = delete all currently-failed stuck rows IN SCOPE so they retry fresh; bulk-ignore = mark them 'ignored') and bulk API endpoints in src/api/server.ts (mutating — use the existing loopback/token authoriseMutation guard), mirroring the single-item /api/stuck/unstick + /api/stuck/ignore semantics EXACTLY: act ONLY on currently-'failed' rows; ignore = the permanent manual-park (off the stuck list, never reprocessed); unstick = delete the ledger row to retry fresh. The popover AND the bulk endpoints must accept an optional SCOPE — all stuck items, a given job, or a given WORKFLOW (resolve to its member jobs) — so the component is reusable from multiple places (Overview now; the Workflows view in T119). Wire it into the Overview page (dashboard/app/page.tsx) — open the popover from the stuck tile/section — and keep the existing per-row controls working. Add unit tests (bulk affects only currently-failed rows, respects job/workflow scope, returns correct counts) and document the popover + bulk controls in CLAUDE.md (stuck/ignored section) + README (Overview).
tasks.doneWhen: A reusable stuck-items popover lists stuck items with per-item Unstick/Ignore and 'Unstick all'/'Ignore all' (confirmed) bulk actions; the bulk endpoints/store act ONLY on currently-failed rows, support an all/job/workflow scope, and return correct counts; the popover is opened from the Overview and the existing per-row controls still work; unit tests cover the bulk operations + scoping; CLAUDE.md + README updated; npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, the dashboard build, and mobile-check are all green.

tasks.title: Backlog: human-reviewed flag on tasks (button, pills, filter, persisted to TASKS.json)
tasks.do: Add a human-review tracking feature so the owner can mark done tasks as checked. (1) DATA: add a `reviewed` boolean to each task in .harness/TASKS.json and BACKFILL every existing task to reviewed:false (safely — preserve every other field, incl. status); treat an absent value as false defensively. (2) API: GET /api/backlog must include each task's `reviewed` (default false); add a MUTATING endpoint POST /api/backlog/:id/reviewed { reviewed: bool } (use the existing loopback/token authoriseMutation guard) that does a SAFE, ATOMIC read-modify-write of .harness/TASKS.json — set ONLY that task's `reviewed` field, preserve all other fields and all other tasks, validate the JSON, and write via temp-file + rename. THIS IS THE FIRST dashboard->harness-file WRITE (the dashboard is otherwise strictly read-only), and the harness loop ALSO read-modify-writes TASKS.json (it sets `status` to done), so the write must be atomic + field-scoped and must not clobber concurrent status edits; confirm the loop's status-write (jq setting .status) preserves the `reviewed` field. (3) UI (Backlog Done section): on each DONE task add a 'Mark as reviewed' toggle button that calls the endpoint; show a coloured 'Reviewed' pill when reviewed:true and a 'Not reviewed' pill when false; add a filter on the Done list for Reviewed / Not reviewed / All. (4) DOCS: document the `reviewed` field + the dashboard write path in CLAUDE.md, .harness/HARNESS.md, and README — explicitly noting this is the deliberate, owner-authorized EXCEPTION to the read-only-dashboard / shell-owns-the-file rules (status remains shell-owned; only `reviewed` is human/dashboard-owned). (5) TESTS: cover the endpoint (sets only the target task's reviewed, preserves all other fields/tasks, atomic, rejects unknown id) and that GET /api/backlog surfaces reviewed.
tasks.doneWhen: Every task in .harness/TASKS.json has a `reviewed` boolean (all backfilled to false with all other fields preserved); GET /api/backlog returns `reviewed`; POST /api/backlog/:id/reviewed atomically sets ONLY that task's `reviewed` (preserving all other fields/tasks, validating before write, loopback/token-guarded); on /backlog each Done task has a 'Mark as reviewed' toggle, a coloured 'Reviewed' vs 'Not reviewed' pill, and the Done list has a Reviewed/Not-reviewed/All filter; the harness loop's status edits preserve `reviewed`; CLAUDE.md + HARNESS.md + README document the field and the dashboard-write exception; unit tests cover the endpoint + GET; npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, the dashboard build, and mobile-check are all green.

tasks.title: Harness: move task do/doneWhen into per-task Markdown files referenced from TASKS.json
tasks.do: Restructure the harness so each task's `do` and `doneWhen` live in a per-task MARKDOWN file referenced from TASKS.json — more expressive than flat JSON strings, and far easier to render on the backlog. DECISIONS (interview 2026-06-23): (a) MIGRATE ALL existing tasks now and make the MD the SOLE source — REMOVE `do` and `doneWhen` from TASKS.json entirely; (b) each MD file has TWO sections, '## Do' and '## Done when'; (c) files live at .harness/tasks/TNNN.md, referenced by an explicit `spec` field on each JSON task object. These changes are COUPLED — removing do/doneWhen from JSON breaks the loop AND the backlog unless they ALL land together, so implement all of: (1) MIGRATION — for every existing task create .harness/tasks/TNNN.md containing '## Do' + the do text then '## Done when' + the doneWhen text (verbatim, NO rewriting — just push the content in), add `spec: ".harness/tasks/TNNN.md"` to its JSON object, and REMOVE the do/doneWhen fields from JSON via a safe atomic field-scoped rewrite that PRESERVES every other field (status, dependsOn, model, gate, reviewed, etc.) and validates the JSON. (2) LOOP — update .harness/loop.sh (and any prompt-building helper) so the per-task PROMPT reads do/doneWhen from the referenced spec MD, while STILL reading every orchestration field (id, status, dependsOn, gate, model, effort, escalation, scope, tags, verify, design) from TASKS.json; the prompt content must match what the loop fed before. (3) BACKLOG API — GET /api/backlog reads each task's spec MD and returns its content (the Do + Done-when sections). (4) BACKLOG PAGE — render the task MD (reuse the T115 markdown renderer if landed) instead of flat do/doneWhen strings. (5) DOCS + CONVENTION — update .harness/HARNESS.md §8 schema + CLAUDE.md for the new shape (do/doneWhen in .harness/tasks/TNNN.md as ## Do / ## Done when; `spec` path in JSON; loop feeds the MD; all else stays JSON), and update the backlog-authoring convention so a NEW task = a JSON object with a `spec` field PLUS a matching MD file (no do/doneWhen in JSON). (6) TESTS — cover the loop building a prompt from the MD and the backlog API returning MD content. If this genuinely can't fit one context window, record a worklog note proposing a split (e.g. migration+loop first, then API+rendering) rather than half-applying. Harmonise with the other backlog-page tasks (T115 markdown render, T123 buildable pill, T124 reviewed flag, T130 difficulty pill).
tasks.doneWhen: Every task's do/doneWhen content lives in .harness/tasks/TNNN.md (two sections: ## Do / ## Done when), each JSON task has a `spec` path to its file, and do/doneWhen no longer appear in TASKS.json (all other fields preserved); .harness/loop.sh builds each task's prompt from the spec MD while reading all orchestration fields from JSON; GET /api/backlog returns the MD content and the backlog page renders it as readable markdown (not a flat string); HARNESS.md §8 + CLAUDE.md document the new shape + authoring convention; unit tests cover the loop prompt-from-MD and the backlog API; the loop still selects + builds tasks end-to-end; npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, and the dashboard build are all green.
